infobox.key: Confederation of Shipbuilding and Engineering Unions
infobox.value: (CSEU), often known as the **Confed** is a [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") [confederation](/wiki/Union_federation "Union federation") in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom").

infobox.key: Federation of Engineering and Shipbuilding Trades
infobox.value: by small [craft unions](/wiki/Craft_union "Craft union"), on the initiative of [Robert Knight](/wiki/Robert_Knight_(trade_unionist) "Robert Knight (trade unionist)") of the [United Society of Boilermakers and Iron and Steel Shipbuilders](/wiki/United_Society_of_Boilermakers_and_Iron_and_Steel_Shipbuilders "United Society of Boilermakers and Iron and Steel Shipbuilders"), primarily in response to the formation of a National Federation of Shipbuilders and Engineers by employers.[[1]](#cite_note-1) By 1895, sixteen unions were affiliated, with a total membership of 150,000.[[2]](#cite_note-tracey-2) However, the prominent [Amalgamated Society of Engineers](/wiki/Amalgamated_Engineering_and_Electrical_Union "Amalgamated Engineering and Electrical Union") (ASE) refused to join.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The ASE finally joined in 1905 but, failing to persuade the other members to unite with it in a single [industrial union](/wiki/Industrial_union "Industrial union"), withdrew again in 1914.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Meanwhile, unions representing unskilled workers were initially excluded; the [National Amalgamated Union of Labour](/wiki/National_Amalgamated_Union_of_Labour "National Amalgamated Union of Labour") was finally admitted in 1908, and the [National Union of Gasworkers and General Labourers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Gasworkers_and_General_Labourers "National Union of Gasworkers and General Labourers") in 1910, and only after they had given assurances that they would permit the craft unions to retain a leading role in the organisation. Because it would not sign a similar agreement, the [Workers' Union](/wiki/Workers%27_Union "Workers' Union") was never admitted.[[5]](#cite_note-5) In its early years, the Federation focussed on resolving demarcation disputes,[[2]](#cite_note-tracey-2) but it soon concentrated on making national agreements for the engineering and shipbuilding industries, allowing the largest union in each trade to take the lead in negotiations.[[6]](#cite_note-marsh-6)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

UK trade union confederation founded 1890

CSEU

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Confederation of Shipbuilding and Engineering Unions | |
|  | |
| Founded | 1890 |
| Headquarters | Salamanca Place, [London](/wiki/London "London") SE1, England |
| Location | * [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") |
| Members | Steady 4 unions |
| General Secretary | Ian Waddell |
| Website | [cseu.org.uk](https://www.cseu.org.uk/) |

The **Confederation of Shipbuilding and Engineering Unions** (CSEU), often known as the **Confed** is a [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") [confederation](/wiki/Union_federation "Union federation") in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Confederation_of_Shipbuilding_and_Engineering_Unions&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The confederation was founded in December 1890 as the **Federation of Engineering and Shipbuilding Trades** by small [craft unions](/wiki/Craft_union "Craft union"), on the initiative of [Robert Knight](/wiki/Robert_Knight_(trade_unionist) "Robert Knight (trade unionist)") of the [United Society of Boilermakers and Iron and Steel Shipbuilders](/wiki/United_Society_of_Boilermakers_and_Iron_and_Steel_Shipbuilders "United Society of Boilermakers and Iron and Steel Shipbuilders"), primarily in response to the formation of a National Federation of Shipbuilders and Engineers by employers.[[1]](#cite_note-1) By 1895, sixteen unions were affiliated, with a total membership of 150,000.[[2]](#cite_note-tracey-2) However, the prominent [Amalgamated Society of Engineers](/wiki/Amalgamated_Engineering_and_Electrical_Union "Amalgamated Engineering and Electrical Union") (ASE) refused to join.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The ASE finally joined in 1905 but, failing to persuade the other members to unite with it in a single [industrial union](/wiki/Industrial_union "Industrial union"), withdrew again in 1914.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Meanwhile, unions representing unskilled workers were initially excluded; the [National Amalgamated Union of Labour](/wiki/National_Amalgamated_Union_of_Labour "National Amalgamated Union of Labour") was finally admitted in 1908, and the [National Union of Gasworkers and General Labourers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Gasworkers_and_General_Labourers "National Union of Gasworkers and General Labourers") in 1910, and only after they had given assurances that they would permit the craft unions to retain a leading role in the organisation. Because it would not sign a similar agreement, the [Workers' Union](/wiki/Workers%27_Union "Workers' Union") was never admitted.[[5]](#cite_note-5) In its early years, the Federation focussed on resolving demarcation disputes,[[2]](#cite_note-tracey-2) but it soon concentrated on making national agreements for the engineering and shipbuilding industries, allowing the largest union in each trade to take the lead in negotiations.[[6]](#cite_note-marsh-6)

The confederation adopted its current form and name in 1936.[[6]](#cite_note-marsh-6) From 1941, the CSEU co-operated with the [National Union of Foundry Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Foundry_Workers "National Union of Foundry Workers") and the [Amalgamated Engineering Union](/wiki/Amalgamated_Engineering_Union "Amalgamated Engineering Union") in the National Engineering Joint Trades Movement, convincing these two unions to affiliate in 1944 and 1946 respectively. The confederation then represented the vast majority of unionised workers in the relevant industries.[[7]](#cite_note-doherty-7)

By 1977, the CSEU had 23 affiliates and 2.4 million members, with most workers organised in the relevant sections of the [Transport and General Workers Union](/wiki/Transport_and_General_Workers_Union "Transport and General Workers Union"), [General and Municipal Workers Union](/wiki/General_and_Municipal_Workers_Union "General and Municipal Workers Union") and [Electrical, Electronic, Telecommunications and Plumbing Union](/wiki/Electrical,_Electronic,_Telecommunications_and_Plumbing_Union "Electrical, Electronic, Telecommunications and Plumbing Union").[[6]](#cite_note-marsh-6) Members in 1979 were:[[8]](#cite_note-8)

* [Amalgamated Society of Boilermakers, Shipwrights, Blacksmiths and Structural Workers](/wiki/Amalgamated_Society_of_Boilermakers,_Shipwrights,_Blacksmiths_and_Structural_Workers "Amalgamated Society of Boilermakers, Shipwrights, Blacksmiths and Structural Workers")
* [Amalgamated Union of Engineering Workers](/wiki/Amalgamated_Union_of_Engineering_Workers "Amalgamated Union of Engineering Workers") (all four sections held separate memberships)
* [Associated Metalworkers' Union](/wiki/Associated_Metalworkers%27_Union "Associated Metalworkers' Union")
* [Association of Patternmakers and Allied Craftsmen](/wiki/Association_of_Patternmakers_and_Allied_Craftsmen "Association of Patternmakers and Allied Craftsmen")
* [Association of Professional, Executive, Clerical and Computer Staff](/wiki/Association_of_Professional,_Executive,_Clerical_and_Computer_Staff "Association of Professional, Executive, Clerical and Computer Staff")
* [Association of Scientific, Technical and Managerial Staffs](/wiki/Association_of_Scientific,_Technical_and_Managerial_Staffs "Association of Scientific, Technical and Managerial Staffs")
* [Electrical, Electronic, Telecommunications and Plumbing Union](/wiki/Electrical,_Electronic,_Telecommunications_and_Plumbing_Union "Electrical, Electronic, Telecommunications and Plumbing Union")
* [Furniture, Timber and Allied Trades Union](/wiki/Furniture,_Timber_and_Allied_Trades_Union "Furniture, Timber and Allied Trades Union")
* [General and Municipal Workers' Union](/wiki/General_and_Municipal_Workers%27_Union "General and Municipal Workers' Union")
* [National Society of Metal Mechanics](/wiki/National_Society_of_Metal_Mechanics "National Society of Metal Mechanics")
* [National Union of Domestic Appliance and General Metal Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Domestic_Appliance_and_General_Metal_Workers "National Union of Domestic Appliance and General Metal Workers")
* [National Union of Scalemakers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Scalemakers "National Union of Scalemakers")
* [National Union of Sheet Metal Workers, Coppersmiths, Heating and Domestic Engineers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Sheet_Metal_Workers,_Coppersmiths,_Heating_and_Domestic_Engineers "National Union of Sheet Metal Workers, Coppersmiths, Heating and Domestic Engineers")
* [Screw, Nut, Bolt and Rivet Trade Union](/wiki/Screw,_Nut,_Bolt_and_Rivet_Trade_Union "Screw, Nut, Bolt and Rivet Trade Union")
* [Transport and General Workers' Union](/wiki/Transport_and_General_Workers%27_Union "Transport and General Workers' Union") (Power & Engineering and Automotive Trade Groups)
* [Union of Construction, Allied Trades and Technicians](/wiki/Union_of_Construction,_Allied_Trades_and_Technicians "Union of Construction, Allied Trades and Technicians")

In 1989, membership was still 22 affiliates and 2 million members but, by 2001, this had fallen to 1.2 million members,[[7]](#cite_note-doherty-7) and a process of union mergers has greatly reduced the number of affiliated unions and the prominence of the organisation. All current affiliates are also members of the [Trades Union Congress](/wiki/Trades_Union_Congress "Trades Union Congress").

By 2017 the union had no direct members and was functioning as a federation of the Unite, GMB, Community, Prospect and UCATT trade unions.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Since the merger of UCATT into Unite the number of member unions has fallen to four.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Current members

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Confederation_of_Shipbuilding_and_Engineering_Unions&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Current members")]

* [Community](/wiki/Community_(trade_union) "Community (trade union)")[[11]](#cite_note-structure-11)
* [GMB](/wiki/GMB_(trade_union) "GMB (trade union)")[[11]](#cite_note-structure-11)
* [Prospect](/wiki/Prospect_(trade_union) "Prospect (trade union)")[[11]](#cite_note-structure-11)
* [Unite the Union](/wiki/Unite_the_Union "Unite the Union")[[11]](#cite_note-structure-11)

## General Secretaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Confederation_of_Shipbuilding_and_Engineering_Unions&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: General Secretaries")]

:   1890: [William Mosses](/wiki/William_Mosses "William Mosses")
:   1917: Frank Smith
:   1937: Ernest Gilbert
:   1942: Gavin Martin
:   1957: George Barratt
:   1970: Jack Service
:   1978: Alex Ferry
:   1994: Alan Robson
:   2004: John Wall
:   2008: Dave Gibbs
:   2009: Hugh Scullion
:   2017: Ian Waddell
